summary: Iteration 6 frontend-only retest after Phase 5 changes (word_connect now has ≥4 words on L1 → 10 by L91; WordCircle 260px fits mobile; MusicPlayer probes /music/track-N.mp3 with procedural fallback; word_search untouched). All 5 review items PASSED. (1) /level/1 'Familija' KAMARO: crossword renders exactly 4 unique words via data-cell-word — AKO, KAM, MAR, ROM (matches expected auto-derivation from MASTER_VOCAB). Letters rendered K,A,R,O,M,A (shuffled per level.id). Tapped circle-letter-2(R), 3(O), 4(M) → preview 'ROM', clicked word-submit → 3 crossword cells filled (R/O/M visible, cell (4,1) is shared with MAR/ROM intersection). progress-label='1/4'. Then tapped K,A,M → KAM filled, progress='2/4'. Screenshot confirms both words solved on board. (2) /level/11 (AMAROD heart): 4 unique words on board (MAR, DARA, DAR, ROM) ≥4 ✓. (3) /level/91 (ROMANIPENESA heart): 10 unique words (NAM,MIRO,AMEN,RAI,MARO,MAR,ROM,MORE,MAS,RAS) hits the target=10 ceiling ✓. (4) Mobile viewport 414×800: docH=800 == winH=800, ZERO overflow. WordCircle, crossword, hint buttons (Cokano/+50/-100), 2/4 progress bar, music FAB ALL visible without scrolling — screenshot attached. (5) /level/6 word_search: grid renders, 10 word-list items present — flow unchanged from previous iterations. Zero console errors during the run.
critical_code_review_comments: wordConnectLevels.js — clean separation of MASTER_VOCAB + canForm + subwords. targetWordsForBlock(blockIdx)=min(10, 4 + floor(blockIdx/1.5)) produces 4,4,5,6,6,7,8,8,9,10 across blocks 0..9. Verified L1 picks 4 of 10 candidate subwords (shortest-first), L91 picks 10. Safety fallback if <3 picked is sensible.

WordCircle.jsx — size=260 with max-w-[260px] sm:max-w-[300px], letterR adaptive (N≤4→26, ≤6→22, ≤8→19, else 16). 6-letter pool on L1 uses r=22 which is comfortably tappable on 414px viewport. Submit button (OK) centered in circle.

MusicPlayer.jsx — probeTrack with /music/track-N.mp3 detection + procedural Gypsy fallback was not directly auto-tested (browser autoplay policy requires user gesture; per agent_to_agent_context_note, expected). README confirms no real tracks shipped, so procedural path is what production preview will hit until tracks added.

CrosswordBoard.jsx — data-cell-word exposes comma-separated list of intersecting words. Verified at L1 cell (4,1) shows 'MAR,ROM' (intersection point), and ROM/KAM/AKO cells each track their owning word. Excellent for deterministic test queries — eliminates need to hard-code grid positions.

Minor (non-blocking): /level/6 word_search uses a CSS-grid render that doesn't expose individual cell data-testids in the form 'ws-cell-r-c' (queried, returned 0). It renders via data-testid='word-search-grid' or similar and word-list-item-* testids are present. If future tests need cell-level drag verification, adding data-testid='ws-cell-{r}-{c}' on each cell would be helpful. Not a bug, just a testability nit.
seed_data_creation: Set localStorage.romword_onboarded=1 and localStorage.romword_progress_v1={coins:200,completed:[],lastVisitedLevel:1} before navigation to bypass onboarding modal.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Critical L1 fact: pool='KAMARO' (6 letters K,A,M,A,R,O) auto-derives EXACTLY 4 words via subwords() from MASTER_VOCAB: AKO,KAM,MAR,ROM (sorted shortest-first, target=4). Circle letters are shuffled per level.id — on this build the rendered order is K,A,R,O,M,A but DO NOT rely on this; always read circle-letter-N text content. Letter dedup rule confirmed: each circle slot used at most once per word, so AKO uses one of the two 'A' slots. Crossword cell selector pattern: [data-testid='crossword-cell-{r}-{c}'] each carries data-cell-word='WORD1,WORD2' (comma-separated when multiple words intersect that cell). Progress label selector: [data-testid='progress-label']. For mobile fit testing, 414×800 produces docH==winH==800 on L1 with shape='rect' — other shapes (heart, diamond, star, plus, rhombus) may render slightly taller crosswords; spot-check if shape masks change. /music/ folder still has only README.txt → MusicPlayer will use procedural fallback. Browser autoplay policy means MusicPlayer audio context only starts after a user gesture (tap on music FAB) — expected, not a bug.
rca of the issue: No bugs found — all 5 Phase-5 review items pass. (1) Word-count growth: subwords() correctly enumerates MASTER_VOCAB-matching subwords per pool; targetWordsForBlock() formula yields the documented 4→10 progression and was empirically verified at the endpoints (L1=4 words, L91=10 words). (2) Mobile fit: shrinking size from previous to 260px brings docH exactly to viewport height on 414×800 — single screen, no scrolling required. (3) Procedural music fallback path is the active path in web preview and produces no console errors. (4) Word_search untouched and continues to render on L6 with the original word list. (5) Crossword placer with seeded mulberry32 produces stable, deterministic placements; testids and data-cell-word attribute enable position-independent verification, which is how this iteration's tests were written.
